title: Chain tip TOCTOU in sync-lag guard undocumented
description: send_payment() fetches chain_tip at one await point, calls check_lag(), then fetches spendable_notes() at a second await point. The chain can advance between the two awaits. On mainnet (75s blocks) this window is bounded, and the 10-block MAX_SYNC_LAG buffer absorbs it. On testnet (fast blocks) the gap can grow larger. The check is security-by-obscurity — it passes but is stale by the time notes are selected. This is a known architectural limitation but is not documented anywhere in the code. A future contributor who tightens the lag threshold (e.g., to 3 blocks) without understanding the TOCTOU window will make the check flakier. Add a comment in send_payment() and sync_guard.rs explaining the race and why the 10-block buffer is load-bearing.
close_reason: Added TOCTOU race explanation to MAX_SYNC_LAG constant in sync_guard.rs (explains the window, why 10-block buffer is load-bearing, what not to do). Added a cross-reference comment in payment.rs at the check_lag call site pointing to that analysis.
